message:
feat(tui): Add codon frequency mode toggle to bias decomposition overlay

Extend the BiasDecompositionOverlay with the ability to switch between
dinucleotide (16-element) and codon (64-element) frequency vectors for
PCA visualization, enabling comparison of both composition signatures.

New Features:
- Mode toggle state: 'di' (dinucleotide) or 'codon' mode
- Keyboard shortcuts: Press '1' for dinucleotide, '2' for codon mode
- Dynamic title: Shows "DINUCLEOTIDE" or "CODON" based on current mode
- Help text updated: "Esc to close · 1=di 2=codon"

Implementation Changes:
- Added mode useState hook with 'di' default
- Imported computeCodonFrequencies from @phage-explorer/core
- Extended LoadedVector interface with optional codon?: number[] property
- Modified data loading to compute both dinucleotide and codon vectors
- Updated decomposition useMemo to select vector based on mode:
  - mode === 'di': Uses v.vector (16-element dinucleotide)
  - mode === 'codon': Uses v.codon ?? v.vector (64-element codon)
- Title text dynamically reflects current analysis mode

Use Cases:
- Dinucleotide mode: General compositional signature comparison
- Codon mode: Host adaptation and codon usage bias analysis
- Switching modes reveals different clustering patterns based on
  different levels of sequence composition analysis

The codon mode produces a higher-dimensional PCA (64 vs 16 features)
which may reveal finer-grained distinctions in coding region preferences
that are averaged out in dinucleotide analysis.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
